title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Maritime Safety, Definition, importance, and scope of
maritime safety. Historical development and evolution of maritime safety
practices, Detailed study of IMO conventions: SOLAS, MARPOL, STCW,
ISPS, and MLC.
Major maritime disasters throughout history, Lessons learned from accidents,
Overview of the International Safety Management (ISM) Code. The role of
Flag States, Port State Control, and Classification Societies.

title: Module 2
content:
Introduction to FSS Code, Purpose of FSS code, General, International Shore
Connections, Personnel Protection, Fire Extinguishers, Fixed gas fire
extinguishing Systems, Fixed foam fire extinguishing systems, Low-location
lighting systems, Fixed emergency fire pumps, Arrangement of means of
escape , Fixed deck foam systems, Inert gas systems.
Fire Test Procedures (FTP), Introduction to Fire Test Procedures, Purpose of
FTP, Relevant SOLAS regulations and standards, Type approval, Types of
fire tests, Non-combustibility test, Smoke and toxicity test, Test for "A", "B"
and "F" class divisions, Test for fire door control systems, Test for surface
flammability, Test for vertically supported textiles and films, Test for
upholstered furniture, Test for bedding components, Test for fire-restricting
materials for high-speed craft, Test for fire-resisting divisions of high-speed
craft.

title: Module 3
content:
Navigation and Collision Avoidance, Principles of Navigation, Celestial
navigation, Electronic navigation systems (ECDIS, GPS, radar), Chartwork
and publications, Collision Avoidance Rules, International Regulations for
Preventing Collisions at Sea (COLREGs), Maneuvering characteristics of
ships, Collision avoidance techniques, Search and Rescue (SAR) Operations,
SAR principles and procedures, Maritime search patterns, SAR equipment
and technology.
Marine Structures and Stability, Ship Hydrostatics and Stability, Buoyancy,
stability, and trim, Inclining experiment, Damage stability and survivability,
Marine Materials and Corrosion, Properties and selection of marine
materials, Corrosion mechanisms and prevention, Fatigue analysis and
design, Offshore Structures, Types of offshore structures (fixed, floating, and
subsea), Environmental loads and design considerations, Structural integrity
and inspection.

title: Module 4
content:
Marine Pollution and Environmental Protection, Marine Pollution Sources
and Types, Oil spills, chemical pollution, and solid waste, Marine pollution
prevention and control, International Maritime Pollution Treaties, MARPOL
Convention and its annexes, Port State Control, Environmental Impact
Assessment (EIA), Principles and methodology of EIA, Environmental
management systems (EMS). Maritime Security and Counter-Terrorism,
Maritime Security Threats, Piracy, armed robbery, and terrorism , Maritime
domain awareness, International Maritime Security Conventions, ISPS Code
and its requirements, Maritime security measures, Counter-Terrorism
Measures, Detection and prevention of terrorist acts, Security drills and
exercises
